title: Irish Lamb Toast
description: A hearty and nostalgic Irish-inspired dish featuring lamb and toasted bread.
instructions: Thaw puff pastry according to package instructions.
In a pan, cook ground lamb over medium-high heat until browned, breaking up into small pieces.
Add marmalade, whiskey (if using), and salt to the lamb. Stir to combine.
Roll out puff pastry to a thickness of about 1/8 inch. Cut into quarters.
Spoon a quarter of the lamb mixture onto one half of each pastry quarter, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Fold the other half of the pastry over the lamb mixture, pressing edges to seal.
Place the sealed pastries on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the tops of the pastries with a little water and sprinkle with salt.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
